about.title: About Ledger
about.tagline: A portfolio tracker that manages domestic and international stocks in real time — on one screen.
about.feature1: Real-time prices for US (USD) and Korean (KRW) stocks simultaneously
about.feature2: Automatic average cost and realized gain calculation from transaction history
about.feature4: Personalized news feed for your holdings
about.feature5: All data stays in your browser — nothing is sent to a server
about.whyBody: No existing app handled both Korean and international stocks accurately in one place, so we built our own. Privacy-first: everything runs in your browser.
about.dataBody: US prices: Finnhub · Earnings calendar: Alpha Vantage · Korean prices & news: Naver Finance

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.noCollectionBody: Ledger does not collect any personal information — no names, emails, or payment details.
privacy.localDataBody: Your holdings, transaction history, and snapshots are stored only in your browser's localStorage. This data never leaves your device and is never sent to any server.
privacy.apisBody: Ledger uses Finnhub, Alpha Vantage, and Naver Finance APIs to fetch real-time prices and news. Ticker symbols you hold may be transmitted to these services. Please refer to their respective privacy policies.
privacy.cookiesBody: Ledger does not use cookies. Only your language preference (Korean/English) is saved in localStorage.
privacy.contactBody: For privacy-related questions, please reach out via GitHub Issues.

help.title: Help
help.gettingStartedBody: Go to the Dashboard and enter a buy transaction in the form. Search by company name to auto-fill the current price.
help.sellBody: Switch to the 'Sell' tab in the transaction form, select a holding, and enter the quantity and sale price. Realized gain is calculated automatically.
help.calendarBody: The Calendar page automatically shows upcoming earnings dates for your USD holdings. You can also add events manually.
help.backupBody: Use the 'Export' button at the bottom of the Dashboard to save a JSON file. If your browser data is cleared, use 'Import' to restore it.
help.faq1A: Browser cache clearing can delete localStorage data. We recommend exporting a backup regularly.
help.faq2Q: Are Korean stock prices updated after market close?
help.faq2A: Korean prices are fetched in real time during market hours only. After close, the last traded price is shown.
help.faq3Q: Can I use Ledger on multiple devices?
help.faq3A: Each device stores data independently for now. Cross-device sync is planned for a future update.

notFound.title: Page Not Found
notFound.body: The page you requested doesn't exist or has been moved.
